# Plays Library v2

Atomic, composable beats for immersive experiences. Designed for agent-assisted experience planning.

---

## How to Use This File (Agent Planning Notes)

Each play has a structured tag block followed by implementation prose. Use the tags to filter candidates; use the prose to plan execution.

**Planning workflow:**
1. Filter by `ARC_FIT` for the current beat position
2. Filter by `AUTONOMY` given operational constraints
3. Filter by `LEAD_TIME` given time available
4. Cross-reference `MECHANISMS` against the participant's mechanism profile (from drivermap)
5. Check `PARTICIPANT_NOTES` against the participant's dimensional profile
6. Use `SYNERGIZES_WITH` to build compound beats; check `CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER` for antipatterns
7. Check `FRAME_REQUIREMENT` — naive-requirement plays misfire after the participant has been exposed to the mechanic
8. Balance `BEAT_FUNCTION` across adjacent plays — avoid adjacent spikes; include rest beats after sustained high-intensity sequences
9. Check `DWELL_TIME` against scheduling bandwidth — don't stack multiple sustained plays simultaneously
10. Note `FEEDBACK_TYPE` against operational capacity — responsive plays require human availability

**Tag definitions:**

`COST` tiers: `free` · `low` (<$25) · `mid` ($25–100) · `high` ($100–500) · `ongoing` (recurring)

`AUTONOMY`: `agent` (fully automated) · `human_assist` (agent prepares, human does one action) · `human_managed` (requires ongoing human involvement) · `confederate` (requires recruiting a real person)

`LEAD_TIME`: days of setup required before deployment

`ARC_FIT`: `pre` (setup before first contact) · `open` (act 1 first contact) · `build` (early investigation) · `escalate` (mid-arc raise stakes) · `threshold` (decision/consent gate) · `climax` (arc peak) · `denouement` (landing) · `any`

`BEAT_FUNCTION`: the narrative role this play serves in the arc's rhythm. `spike` — sharp emotional peak, maximum arousal, not sustained (revelation, confrontation). `ramp` — builds tension or anticipation toward a future beat. `hold` — maintains sustained engagement at a particular register without escalating. `rest` — recovery; lets the participant integrate before the next escalation. `transition` — shifts emotional or epistemic register without spiking or resting. Arc design requires alternating beat functions: adjacent spikes flatten; rest without a preceding ramp is aimless. From Imagineering contrast principle (Joe Rohde): every element has meaning only in relation to the element before it. Multiple values allowed.

`INTENSITY`: `low` · `medium` · `high` · `extreme`

`SOMATIC`: physiological state the play induces · what state the participant needs to be in for the play to land correctly. Format: `induces: [state] · requires: [state]`. Plays deployed without regard for somatic sequencing misfire. `simulated_loss` dropped cold is destabilizing; after sustained trust-building it is cathartic. Somatic sequencing is a design constraint, not an afterthought.

`IDENTITY_INVITE`: the self the participant gets to try on via this play. Experiences are identity-editing tools — temporary access to a self not available in ordinary life. Each play extends an implicit invitation; knowing what it is lets you sequence toward a coherent identity arc. (Source: Raj Samuel, WXO LXW26 #53.)

`REINFORCE`: follow-on touchpoints that extend the play's effect into daily life after the beat ends. Based on the 3R model: Revelation (the play itself) → Reinforcement (what arrives in days/weeks after) → Replication (behavioral shift appearing unprompted). The denouement is not the end of operator responsibility — it is the start of the reinforcement phase. (Source: Richard Maddock, WXO LXW26 #66.)

`PERMISSION`: whether the play grants or requires prior permission to engage, and whether it can stand alone or must follow a permission-granting play. Format: `grants/requires [type] · standalone/sequenced`. Guests seek permission from other humans (and human-feeling systems) before they will actually immerse. Plays that arrive before permission is granted are dismissed; plays that grant permission make subsequent plays land. (Source: Terence Leclere, WXO LXW26 #31.)

`EMOTIONAL_REGISTER`: the qualitative affect the play produces — emotional color, not intensity level. Values: `wonder` · `dread` · `grief` · `urgency` · `warmth` · `awe` · `shame` · `disorientation` · `triumph` · `longing` · `paranoia` · `tenderness`. Arc design requires balancing registers across beats; identical registers in sequence flatten. Distinct from SOMATIC (physiological) and INTENSITY (arousal level).

`AGENCY_DEMAND`: how much active participation the play requires from the participant. `passive` — participant receives without needing to act. `low` — requires minor decision or acknowledgment. `medium` — requires deliberate action (going somewhere, solving something). `high` — requires sustained effort across time (multi-step investigation, maintaining a character relationship). High-agency participants disengage from passive-only arcs; low-agency participants fatigue under stacked high-demand plays.

`FEEDBACK_TYPE`: what kind of response the participant receives for their actions or presence. `none` — the play delivers without any feedback loop; participant actions do not change what arrives. `ambient` — the environment responds to participant presence but not to specific choices. `diegetic` — in-world feedback that makes narrative sense; the system appears to respond in-character. `responsive` — direct, personalized, real-time response to specific participant actions or statements. Feedback type affects expectation-setting: responsive plays that fail to respond feel broken; none plays that appear responsive feel deceptive. Match feedback type to operational capacity.

`DETECTION_WINDOW`: how long before a reasonably attentive participant will recognize this beat as constructed. `immediate` (hours) · `short` (1–3 days) · `medium` (1–2 weeks) · `long` (weeks–months) · `never_solo` (undetectable without external disclosure). Affects arc pacing — a window-collapsing play deployed too early ends the implicit frame. Some windows are features (investigation plays are meant to be found); others are operational risks.

`REVERSIBILITY`: how easily this play can be walked back if the participant uses a safety word, shows genuine distress, or the experience ends. `trivial` (cancel/ignore, no trace) · `easy` (one action removes it) · `difficult` (requires active cleanup, leaves residue) · `irreversible` (permanent perceptual or world-state change). Plan your commitment surface before deploying. Safety apparatus requires knowing exit options before entry.

`CHANNEL`: primary delivery medium. `physical_mail` · `phone` · `email` · `in_person` · `digital_ambient` (web, social, search) · `physical_world` (objects, locations) · `multi`. Enables channel saturation planning — avoid stacking more than 2–3 plays in the same channel in one week.

`REQUIRES`: operator resource type beyond money. `cash_only` · `social_capital` (confederate, favor, introduction) · `institutional_access` (business account, credentials, professional identity) · `technical_skill` (voice cloning, deepfake, custom code) · `local_presence` (operator or confederate physically nearby) · `ongoing_maintenance` (monitoring and updates throughout arc, not one-time). Multiple values allowed.

`SYNERGIZES_WITH`: specific play IDs that compound well when deployed in sequence or alongside this play — based on what alibi, mechanism, or somatic state this play creates that another play exploits. Not exhaustive; use to build compound beats.

`CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER`: play IDs that should not immediately precede this play. Running this play after those creates emotional whiplash, mechanism saturation, or epistemic failure. `none` if no strong contraindications known.

`AGENCY_TYPE`: the kind of agency the play constructs, grants, reflects, or removes. Drawn from C. Thi Nguyen's framework of agency-sculpting in games. `constrained` — participant's normal degrees of freedom deliberately narrowed (attention focused, options removed, confusion installed). `constructed` — new agency handed to participant; a role, capability, or mode of attention they didn't have before. `revealed` — existing agency surfaced; participant learns they already had this access, capacity, or history. `suspended` — agency made unreliable or ambiguous; participant can't act on their world with normal confidence. `mirrored` — participant's own choices and history reflected back as a visible pattern with stakes. `borrowed` — agency of a role or institution loaned to participant; they act as heir, operative, insider, or functionary. Multiple values allowed (e.g. `constructed · borrowed`). Sequencing note: arcs that only constrain without constructing produce helplessness not transformation; arcs that only construct without ever constraining produce shallow achievement. The most resonant arcs move through constrained → borrowed/constructed → revealed.

`FRAME_REQUIREMENT`: the epistemic state the participant must be in for this play to function correctly. `naive` — participant must not yet know they're in an experience, or must have no prior exposure to this mechanic type; the play fails if they're looking for it. `primed` — participant should have enough context to engage intentionally but not enough to predict the play's specific form. `meta` — only works, or works best, when the participant is aware of the system and engaging knowingly. `any` — functions regardless of epistemic state. Critical for sequencing: a `naive`-requirement play deployed after the participant recognizes the mechanic type will misfire. Cross-domain deployment (using a mentalism mechanic in a financial document context) can temporarily restore naive conditions for fluent participants.

`DWELL_TIME`: how long this play actively occupies the participant's attention or world after deployment. `instant` — single moment of engagement (seconds to minutes); resolves immediately. `brief` — occupies participant for up to an hour; resolves within the session. `session` — active engagement across a full day. `multi-day` — requires participant attention or creates active presence across several days. `sustained` — ongoing engagement over weeks; part of the ambient texture of the arc. `permanent` — creates a lasting change in how the participant perceives something; never fully resolves. Distinct from LEAD_TIME (setup before deployment). Scheduling constraint: stacking multiple `sustained` plays creates cognitive overload.

`TROPE_RISK`: the degree to which a participant fluent in a particular genre community will immediately recognize and name this mechanic, breaking diegetic processing. Format: `source:level` pairs separated by ` · `, or `none` if no established community has named this. Sources: `arg` (alternate reality game players — anomalous documents, TINAG logic, multi-key puzzles), `escape_room` (lock-and-key logic, cipher-on-surface, examine-everything mode), `immersive_theater` (one-on-one rooms, actor-who-won't-break, following-a-stranger), `mentalism` (Barnum readings, sealed envelopes, forcing, you-chose-freely framing), `spy_fiction` (dead drops, handler letters, challenge-response, tradecraft vocabulary), `cult_recovery` (love bombing, incremental oath, thought-stopping, us-vs-them framing), `puzzle` (general puzzle community, overlap zone). Levels: `high` (instantly named by anyone with moderate exposure), `medium` (recognized by regular participants), `low` (only practitioners or veterans would name it). Design note: route high-trope plays away from fluent participants — cross-domain deployment (a mentalism mechanic delivered as a financial document) can drop effective risk by one level.

### By Trope Risk

### Certified / Registered Mail
`id:certified_mail` · `COST:low` · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:3–5 days` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** significance_quest · obedience_authority · curiosity_exploration (anticipation before opening)
**ARC_FIT:** open · threshold · climax · revelation
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Works for all profiles. With high `bis_sensitivity` participants on persecution arcs, the arrival of certified mail before rapport is established can spike anxiety — build relationship first.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** wax_seal · notarized_doc · informed_delivery_gap · mailbox_address · llc_registration
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** document
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: low-grade alertness, mild institutional-threat activation · requires: baseline; works cold
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person whose affairs have caught the attention of something with legal standing
**REINFORCE:** follow-on certified mail at day 7 elevates pattern; reference the document number in a subsequent voicemail; later communication that cites it by number
**PERMISSION:** grants · standalone
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** urgency
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** spy_fiction:medium · arg:low
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** long
**REVERSIBILITY:** easy
**CHANNEL:** physical_mail
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** cash_only · institutional_access

USPS Certified Mail ($4.35 + postage) requires a signature. That signature is the mechanic — the participant must actively receive it; it becomes an event, not a passive delivery. Nobody sends certified mail casually. Registered Mail ($14+) adds tracking and insurance and moves slower; use when the implied security of the contents matters narratively.

Give the participant a heads-up that "something is coming" via another channel the day before delivery — this creates anticipation that further loads the object.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant not home; held at post office and never retrieved — use a heads-up message and confirm their schedule with sponsor
- Return address raises suspicion if personal — use mailbox service address
- Flat envelope underdelivers the implied weight — use a padded envelope or a substantial packet

---

### USPS Informed Delivery Gap
`id:informed_delivery_gap` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:2 weeks (account setup); 0 after` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** anticipation · duality · priming · behavioral_inhibition_activation · cognitive_dissonance · affective_priming
**ARC_FIT:** any (stack with all physical mail)
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** No concerns. Verify participant is enrolled in Informed Delivery (most urban addresses are).
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** every physical mail play
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: hypervigilance, cognitive dissonance about a system they trust · requires: baseline; most potent after prior beats have primed for surveillance
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person being managed by something that works inside bureaucratic infrastructure
**REINFORCE:** reference the gap in a later communication ("you noticed something was missing"); the effect compounds with any subsequent physical mail
**PERMISSION:** requires participant uses USPS Informed Delivery (verify via intake) · sequenced after establishing the narrative context
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** disorientation · paranoia
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** suspended
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:low
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** short
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** email
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** institutional_access · ongoing_maintenance

USPS Informed Delivery sends participants a morning email with grayscale scans of incoming mail. Mailers can add a full-color "ride-along" digital image (4.75"×2.375" or 4"×6") that appears alongside the scan. This creates two simultaneous versions of the same moment: the digital preview that morning, the physical object hours later.

**Three uses:**
1. Envelope exterior designed as cryptic artifact in grayscale — they see something strange in their morning email before they know what it is
2. Ride-along image that's adjacent or different from physical contents — the digital and physical tell different parts of the story
3. Ride-along as advance primer — the image primes interpretation of the physical piece before it arrives

Setup: USPS Business Customer Gateway account required. Takes ~2 weeks. After setup, ride-along submission is per-mailing job.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant not enrolled in Informed Delivery — confirm with sponsor
- Image spec constraints (small, specific dimensions, 72dpi) — design to spec; test with proof
- Grayscale preview loses color-dependent design — simulate before finalizing

---

### Notarized Document
`id:notarized_doc` · `COST:low` · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** obedience_authority · significance_quest · trust_formation
**ARC_FIT:** open · threshold · escalate
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Effective across all profiles. Especially potent for high `need_for_closure` participants who seek institutional legitimacy.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** certified_mail · llc_registration · mailbox_address · classified_doc_aesthetic
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** document
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: slight institutional unease + respect for legal weight · requires: baseline
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person with legal standing in something larger than themselves
**REINFORCE:** subsequent communications can "cite" the document; any follow-on certified mail carrying related materials builds the stack
**PERMISSION:** grants · standalone
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** urgency · awe
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** spy_fiction:low
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** long
**REVERSIBILITY:** easy
**CHANNEL:** physical_mail
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** cash_only · institutional_access

Notarization verifies the signing event, not the content or the organization. The embossed seal and wet signature are completely real and legally valid. The document can say anything. Most people have never examined a notarized document closely enough to know what to check.

Walk into any UPS Store with the printed document. ~$15, same day.

Paper stock matters: 32lb cream stock reads as institutional; standard 20lb white reads as a printout. Crane & Co. paper if the stakes justify it.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant happens to know how notarization works — acceptable; they still can't fake the seal
- Cheap paper undermines the presentation — invest in paper stock
- Document layout looks like a home printout — design proper letterhead

---

### LLC Registration
`id:llc_registration` · `COST:high` · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:2–5 days` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** social_proof · trust_formation (organizational legitimacy)
**ARC_FIT:** pre
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Background infrastructure. No direct participant interaction.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** mailbox_address · google_maps · press_release · notarized_doc · real_domain
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** document
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark
**SOMATIC:** induces: background hum of legitimacy, mild cognitive dissonance · requires: baseline; most effective when participant investigates the org
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person dealing with an organization that has real public standing
**REINFORCE:** every document using the LLC name builds on this; the record is permanent and findable at any arc moment; real_domain and linkedin_character inherit this legitimacy
**PERMISSION:** grants (passive legitimacy anchor) · pre-deploys before arc begins; must precede all other org-identity plays
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** awe
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** none
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** never_solo
**REVERSIBILITY:** difficult
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** cash_only · institutional_access · ongoing_maintenance

Wyoming (~$100/year) or Delaware (~$90/year). The fictional organization has a real state registration number, articles of incorporation, a registered agent address. Participants who search public business registries find it. This is foundational infrastructure for any experience where the organization needs to hold up to serious scrutiny.

Filing services (Northwest Registered Agent, Registered Agents Inc.) handle everything online. Human needed only for signature.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant searches for financial activity — expected for a new LLC; no public financial filing required
- Name conflicts with existing business — check ahead
- Overkill for experiences under 2 weeks — right-size to arc length

---

### Mailbox Service as Physical Address
`id:mailbox_address` · `COST:ongoing` (~$30–89/month) · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:1–3 days` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** social_proof · trust_formation
**ARC_FIT:** pre
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Background infrastructure.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** llc_registration · google_maps · notarized_doc · real_domain
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark
**SOMATIC:** induces: subtle uncanny — the address works, but the building is hollow · requires: triggered when participant investigates the org
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** investigator finding the edges of a real-seeming organizational structure
**REINFORCE:** any physical mail using this return address; the listing persists; LinkedIn and Google Maps both reference it
**PERMISSION:** grants (passive organizational anchor) · pre-deploys; enables certified_mail, notarized_doc, and other physical-sender plays
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** none
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** never_solo
**REVERSIBILITY:** easy
**CHANNEL:** physical_world
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** cash_only · ongoing_maintenance

Regus virtual office (~$49–89/month) gives a "suite" address in a real building. UPS Store PMB ($20–40/month) is cheaper but the "PMB" designation is recognizable. iPostal1 (~$10–30/month) offers photo verification of mail.

Use Regus for any experience where the participant might scrutinize the address. Regus buildings have real lobbies visible in Street View; suite numbers look like offices.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant physically visits — they'll find a mailbox service; this is deflating but not fatal; the address's *existence* is the main claim
- UPS Store PMB notation visible to anyone who looks — use Regus for high-scrutiny participants

---

### Wax Seal and Custom Embosser
`id:wax_seal` · `COST:low` (setup); free thereafter · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:3–5 days` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** significance_quest · novelty · priming (before opening) · curiosity_exploration
**ARC_FIT:** open · threshold · climax
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Works for all profiles. Particularly effective for high `big_five_O` participants who respond to aesthetic signals of difference.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** certified_mail · notarized_doc · handwritten_letter · informed_delivery_gap
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** document
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: tactile ritual satisfaction + slight solemnity · requires: participant is receiving physical mail and handles it attentively
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** recipient of correspondence from something with gravitas — a tradition, a society, an institution with ceremony
**REINFORCE:** seal on subsequent communications creates a consistent aesthetic vocabulary the participant comes to recognize and anticipate
**PERMISSION:** grants (ceremonial register) · always sequenced after certified_mail or similar physical delivery
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** awe · wonder
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:low · immersive_theater:low
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** immediate
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** physical_mail
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** cash_only

A wax-sealed envelope is categorically different before it's opened. Nobody receives one in ordinary life. The anachronism signals: ordinary rules don't apply here. Custom rubber stamps ($10–20 from any stamp shop or Etsy) take 3–5 days. Wax seal starter kit ($15–25). Hard wax, not flexible glue-gun wax — flexible wax breaks in transit.

Custom embosser (the raised seal type, ~$30–50) is even stronger but takes longer to produce.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Seal breaks in transit — protect with a small piece of bubble wrap inside the outer envelope, or ship in a padded mailer
- Looks DIY — practice the application; uneven wax or rushed imprint undermines the whole effect
- Wrong wax color — black, dark red, or deep green reads as official; craft colors undermine it

---

### Classified Document Aesthetic
`id:classified_doc_aesthetic` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** obedience_authority · significance_quest · curiosity_exploration
**ARC_FIT:** open · build · escalate · threshold
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Especially effective for participants with high `need_for_cognition` and investigative temperament. May feel campy to participants with government/security backgrounds who know real formatting.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** redacted_document · accidental_transmission · notarized_doc · real_domain
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** document
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: mild investigation-mode excitement, the arousal of being cleared to see something · requires: baseline; strongest when participant is in active investigation mode
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person cleared to see what others aren't — an insider by selection
**REINFORCE:** subsequent documents in the same aesthetic build a collection; participant accumulates a visual vocabulary of the organization
**PERMISSION:** grants (investigation-clearance permission) · standalone; strongest after nomination_framing has established why they were selected
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** urgency · awe
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** low
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:medium · spy_fiction:medium
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** medium
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** email
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill

The visual language of classified and official documents carries weight far beyond its informational content. Key elements:

- **Paragraph classification markings:** `(U)` (Unclassified), `(SBU)` (Sensitive But Unclassified), `(FOUO)` (For Official Use Only) before each paragraph
- **Header/footer classification banners:** top and bottom of each page
- **Distribution statement:** "Distribution authorized to [agency] only. Other requests shall be referred to [office]."
- **Document control number:** YYYY-MM-DD-XXXX format
- **NOFORN / ORCON / PROPIN** handling caveats for verisimilitude
- **Originator:** an office that sounds real within the fictional organization
- **"This document contains information affecting national security..."** boilerplate

None of this requires any official sanction. These are formatting conventions. The visual register activates obedience_authority before the content is read.

**Critical:** Don't impersonate real government agencies. Use the fictional organization's name in all official fields. This creates a document that looks classified without claiming to be government material.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Real government/security professional in participant's life who reviews it and notes formatting errors — acceptable; most participants don't have this context
- Overuse makes the game feel like a spy parody — use sparingly, for the moments that need it

---

## INTIMACY & SURVEILLANCE

Plays that create the sensation the experience already knows the participant.

---

### OSINT Personalization
`id:osint_personalization` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:varies`

**MECHANISMS:** hypervigilance · significance_quest · curiosity_exploration
**ARC_FIT:** pre (build profile); any (deploy throughout)
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** The foundational personalization layer for all other plays. Verify everything before use — a wrong detail destroys the effect.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** everything; this is the layer that makes all other plays land
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** identity
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: hypervigilance, the "cold wash" of being known before contact was made · requires: baseline; does not need priming
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person who has been seen and studied before they knew it
**REINFORCE:** every subsequent personalized detail compounds — the first instance creates the effect; later instances prove its persistence; never fully fades
**PERMISSION:** grants (surveillance acceptance) · standalone; also enables and amplifies nearly every other play in the library
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** paranoia · disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** suspended
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:high
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** short
**REVERSIBILITY:** easy
**CHANNEL:** email
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill

Sources: LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram (public), Strava/fitness (if public), Yelp reviews they've written, Nextdoor, Google Maps reviews, voter registration, property records, local newspaper archives, professional license databases, Eventbrite/Meetup RSVPs, alumni directories, PACER for federal court records.

Gather 8–15 specific verified true things. Tier them:
- **Tier 1 (surprising, specific):** a Yelp review they wrote, a specific running route, a comment on Nextdoor, a public event RSVP at an unusual event
- **Tier 2 (confirming, expected):** their neighborhood, their employer, their general interests
- **Tier 3 (ambient):** their city's weather, local news, neighborhood events

Use 2–3 Tier 1 details per message maximum. More reads as surveillance report, not knowledge.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Getting something wrong (outdated info, wrong person's data) — verify every detail before use; a single error is irrecoverable
- Using something that feels private despite being public — calibrate to participant's known privacy orientation (high `big_five_N` participants may be more sensitive)
- Over-saturation — restraint is more effective than volume

---

### Local Area Code Number
`id:local_area_code` · `COST:ongoing` (~$2/month) · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** mere_exposure_effect · trust_formation
**ARC_FIT:** open · any
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Universal. Assign from first contact; maintain throughout.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** ai_phone_agent · voicemail_drop · matched_prefix
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: mild attention-alert + compulsion to answer · requires: baseline
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person receiving a call from their own world — something that knows where they are
**REINFORCE:** same number used for follow-up calls; participants often save the number, which means the narrative has a contact in their address book
**PERMISSION:** standalone
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** paranoia
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** none
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** medium
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** phone
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** cash_only

Buy via Twilio with area code matching participant's city. Assign one number per fictional character for consistency. The number becomes associated with the character across the arc.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Spam detection apps (Hiya, Nomorobo) may flag — check Twilio number reputation before use at lookup.cnam.io
- Participant auto-blocks unfamiliar local numbers — increasingly common; have a text before a call

---

### Voicemail Drop (Ringless)
`id:voicemail_drop` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** significance_quest · hypervigilance · curiosity_exploration
**ARC_FIT:** open · build · escalate
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Effective across profiles. Don't over-use — 2–3 per arc maximum.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** local_area_code · ai_phone_agent · implied_prior_relationship
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** ephemeral
**SOMATIC:** induces: low-arousal confusion + the "did I miss something important" activation · requires: baseline; works cold
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person whose life is being quietly tracked by something patient
**REINFORCE:** a second voicemail referencing the first elevates the pattern; subsequent calls that acknowledge the voicemail was left
**PERMISSION:** standalone; grants low-level sustained-contact permission for subsequent beats
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** urgency · disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** low
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** suspended
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:medium
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** immediate
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** phone
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** cash_only · technical_skill

Services: Slybroadcast, Drop Cowboy, Straight To Voicemail. Deposits voicemail without ringing. Pair with a single-ring Twilio call at the same time for a matching missed-call on caller ID.

The message should sound like a second or third attempt: "I've left you a couple messages. I'm not sure if they're going through." Background ambience (mild office noise) adds authenticity. Message length: 30–60 seconds; shorter messages are more unsettling.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- T-Mobile and some carriers actively block ringless — effectiveness varies; test on participant's carrier first if known
- Participant never checks voicemail — have a text follow-up referencing "the message I left"
- Visual voicemail shows sending service metadata — acceptable for most participants

---

### Real Financial Micro-Transaction
`id:micro_transaction` · `COST:low` ($1–20) · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:2–3 days (verify platform)` · `INTENSITY:high`

**MECHANISMS:** reciprocity · commitment_consistency · reality_threshold_crossing · cognitive_dissonance
**ARC_FIT:** build · threshold
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** High impact across all profiles. The reality threshold effect is universal. Do not use with high `attachment_anxious` participants before rapport is established — unexpected money can read as obligation or manipulation.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** gift_card · nomination_framing · profiling_survey (as payment for "completing the assessment")
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** document
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** ephemeral
**SOMATIC:** induces: jolt of hypervigilance — the financial system, the most trusted and monitored channel, has been penetrated · requires: primed by prior beats; most potent mid-arc
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person whose actual financial life has been touched by the narrative
**REINFORCE:** the transaction record persists in their account history; reference it later ("your account received a transfer on [date]"); participant may screenshot
**PERMISSION:** requires explicit sponsor consent for financial access · standalone if consent granted; never without it
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** disorientation · urgency
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** constructed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:low
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** immediate
**REVERSIBILITY:** difficult
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** cash_only · social_capital

Money crossing from fiction into the participant's real financial history is the strongest reality-threshold play in the library. The note field is the narrative payload. Venmo/Cash App preferred over PayPal (which may hold funds).

Require human execution — most platforms flag automated patterns. Agent prepares the note and timing; human executes.

**Key:** Venmo is public by default. Change to private before sending.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant doesn't use any P2P app — check at intake; have fallback
- Digital gift card in email looks like phishing — physical is preferred for gift card equivalent
- Amount too small reads as payment verification scam — $5–15 is the right range
- PayPal may hold unusual incoming payments

---

### Matched-Prefix Number
`id:matched_prefix` · `COST:ongoing` (~$2/month) · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** hypervigilance · uncanny_recognition
**ARC_FIT:** escalate · threshold · you_ve_been_found moment
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Works for all profiles. Deploy once only — the effect is in the first encounter.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** you_ve_been_found_inversion · ai_phone_agent
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: brief hypervigilance then cognitive dissonance when the number doesn't resolve to anyone they know · requires: primed by a few prior contact beats
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person being called by something that knows their specific world — not just their city, their neighborhood
**REINFORCE:** pairs with subsequent local_area_code calls from the same or similar numbers; the participant's phone becomes a channel they regard differently
**PERMISSION:** grants · standalone possible; most effective following at least one prior communication
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** paranoia · disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** revealed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** none
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** immediate
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** phone
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** cash_only

Same area code AND same 3-digit prefix as participant's own number. Twilio number search supports pattern matching. The double-take this produces is unlike any other phone number effect. A call or text from a number that looks almost like their own reads as the experience reaching into their identity.

**Use once. The effect is entirely in the first encounter.**

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Matching prefix not available in Twilio inventory — common; check early and have a fallback
- Effect too subtle for participants who don't look at incoming numbers carefully

---

### The Implied Prior Relationship
`id:implied_prior_relationship` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** hypervigilance · hot_cold_empathy_gap · curiosity_exploration
**ARC_FIT:** open · build
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Effective across most profiles. Use carefully with high `attachment_anxious` participants — implied prior relationships can activate anxiety rather than curiosity if the tone is wrong.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** accidental_transmission · voicemail_drop · past_tense_timestamp
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** character
**LANDSCAPE:** identity
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: disorientation, epistemological vertigo — "have I forgotten something important?" · requires: baseline; most potent with high need_for_cognition participants
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person re-entering a relationship they don't remember making — someone with a history they're only now discovering
**REINFORCE:** every subsequent communication that assumes prior familiarity deepens the groove; participant builds an involuntary false memory map
**PERMISSION:** grants (implied-consent via confusion) · standalone
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** low
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** revealed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** none
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** short
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** email
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill

An email or message framed as a continuation of something that hasn't happened yet. "Following up on our previous conversation." "I wanted to circle back on what we discussed." "I haven't heard from you since [vague past reference]." "We've been patient."

The participant searches their inbox and finds nothing. The gap between "implied history" and "no record" is disorienting in a generative way. They assume they missed something rather than that nothing happened.

Works best when: the tone is professional and slightly impatient rather than alarming; when it references something plausible but unspecific; when there's something to "follow up" on in the message itself.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Implied prior interaction that can be definitively disproven ("we met at X event" when they weren't there)
- Tone too accusatory — activates anxiety rather than curiosity with high `big_five_N` participants

---

### Planted Object
`id:planted_object` · `COST:low` · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:1–3 days` · `INTENSITY:high`

**MECHANISMS:** hypervigilance · significance_quest · curiosity_exploration · territorial_instinct · proxemics_personal_space
**ARC_FIT:** open · escalate
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Requires physical access to participant's personal space — home, vehicle, workplace. Must have confederate or direct access. Confirm with sponsor that access is possible before committing.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** implied_prior_relationship · past_tense_timestamp · qr_code · voicemail_drop · accidental_transmission
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: hypervigilance, territorial unease — something entered their private space · requires: primed by at least one prior digital beat; arrival into physical space is the escalation
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person whose physical world has been entered — not just their inbox
**REINFORCE:** subsequent contact that "explains" the object completes the circuit; participant may photograph it and send it; it continues to exist in their space
**PERMISSION:** requires sponsor authorization for access to participant's space · sequenced; never without explicit sponsor pre-clearance
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** paranoia · wonder
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** medium
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** constructed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:medium · escape_room:high
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** immediate
**REVERSIBILITY:** easy
**CHANNEL:** physical_world
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** local_presence · social_capital

An unexplained object appears in the participant's personal space. Not at a designated pickup location — in their home, on their car, at their desk. It contains or points to instructions. The message is: we were already here.

The object must be anomalous but mundane enough to be explicable as a mistake — not alarming, just wrong. Options: a sealed envelope with their name, a small package on the doorstep, a book left on their car seat, a wooden figure on their kitchen counter. The object is the assertion of access; its contents are secondary.

The planted object delivers three effects simultaneously: surveillance (we know your space), agency (we chose to enter it), invitation (this was left for you). No other play delivers all three.

Access routes by invasiveness: doorstep (non-entry) → mail slot → neighbor hand-off under cover story → direct placement. The more access the approach requires, the more powerful the effect — but match to consent scope established in intake.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant assumes it's misdelivered mail and discards it — include their name prominently; make it clearly personal
- Object creates genuine alarm — design to be clearly weird but benign; avoid anything that reads as threatening; have a contact number available in or on the object
- Access proves impossible — fall back to `fedex_tracking` with an internal note referencing their address, which achieves partial effect

---

## MYSTERY & BREADCRUMB

Plays that invite investigation and reward the curious.

---

### The Accidental Transmission
`id:accidental_transmission` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:high`

**MECHANISMS:** curiosity_exploration · confirmation_bias (satisfying variant) · significance_quest
**ARC_FIT:** build · escalate · revelation
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** High effectiveness across profiles. Particularly strong for high `need_for_cognition` participants who enter active processing mode on discovery.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** real_domain · linkedin_character · parallel_threads · classified_doc_aesthetic
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** ephemeral
**SOMATIC:** induces: furtive excitement, the overhearing sensation — "I wasn't meant to see this" · requires: baseline; works cold
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person who accidentally found themselves behind a curtain
**REINFORCE:** subsequent "correction" or recall attempt amplifies the sense that something was exposed; follow-up that treats the slip as sensitive
**PERMISSION:** standalone; grants investigation permission naturally without requiring explicit invitation
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** wonder · urgency
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** low
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** constructed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:high
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** short
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** email
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill

The most effective pure-email technique. A message that appears to have been sent to the participant by mistake shifts them from passive receiver to active discoverer. Information that feels *found* is processed as more true and more significant than information that was delivered.

The design surface is **below the fold**. The "accidental" top message can be brief and confusing. The forwarded chain or attached document below is where the narrative content lives. Design every level of the chain — names, timestamps, email domains, tone.

**Forms:**
- Internal memo sent to wrong distribution list — participant is on it by mistake
- Forwarded chain accidentally including them as a recipient
- Document attached to a message clearly intended for someone else
- Reply-all that included them when it shouldn't have

The fictional sender should be contactable (email on real domain) and should respond to any "I think you sent this to the wrong person" reply with appropriate alarm and attempted damage control.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Too obvious — the mistake must be plausible; modern email users are skeptical of dramatically misdirected sensitive documents; make it a plausible operational slip
- Too opaque — comprehensible enough to be compelling, detailed enough to reward attention
- No plan for reply — the participant will often respond; design the character's reaction

---

### Real Domain + Aged Content
`id:real_domain` · `COST:ongoing` (~$15/month) · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:2+ weeks; months for deep aging` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** social_proof · trust_formation · curiosity_exploration
**ARC_FIT:** pre (build); build (participant discovers)
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** hold
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Background infrastructure. Activate when participant is given reason to investigate.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** llc_registration · press_release · linkedin_character · google_maps · annotation_layer
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark
**SOMATIC:** induces: low-level background legitimacy (passive) · requires: participant must be given a reason to visit; domain alone does nothing
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** investigator finding a real trail — not just a message, but an organization with infrastructure
**REINFORCE:** content can age and update over the arc; each visit can reveal more; the site exists before and after the experience and is findable by anyone
**PERMISSION:** grants (investigation mode, org legitimacy) · standalone if domain is seeded; enables the full investigation arc play stack
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** wonder
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** medium
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** ambient
**AGENCY_TYPE:** constructed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:high
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** medium
**REVERSIBILITY:** easy
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** sustained
**REQUIRES:** cash_only · technical_skill · ongoing_maintenance

Domain (~$15/year) + hosting (Netlify/Vercel free tiers work for static sites) + Let's Encrypt SSL (free). The organization holds up to Googling.

**Key elements:**
- Backdated "About" and "News" content (CMSs allow post backdating)
- Team page with AI-generated character faces linked to LinkedIn profiles
- Contact form routing to monitored inbox — they can actually reach the organization
- Wayback Machine saves (archive.org/save) — request early and often to build crawl history
- Subtle inconsistencies that reward deep investigation — don't make it perfect

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Whois shows recent registration — use domain privacy on all registrars
- Too polished (template-obvious) or too DIY (clearly built in a day) — aim for small-professional-organization register
- Contact form dead inbox — someone must monitor and respond in character

---

### Classified Ad in Local Newspaper
`id:classified_ad` · `COST:low` · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:3–7 days` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** curiosity_exploration · significance_quest (discovery variant)
**ARC_FIT:** build · escalate
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Only effective for participants directed to look, or for participants likely to encounter it organically (heavy local paper readers).
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** physical_dead_drop · incomplete_sequence · nomination_framing
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark
**SOMATIC:** induces: investigative excitement + slight surrealism — the most archaic channel deployed with precision · requires: participant in investigation mode or with motivation to read local classifieds
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person who found what the ad was placed for them to find — the intended recipient
**REINFORCE:** a second ad in the same publication at a later date creates a pattern; response mechanism in the ad enables escalation
**PERMISSION:** grants · sequenced after narrative context is established
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** wonder · urgency
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** medium
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:medium
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** long
**REVERSIBILITY:** easy
**CHANNEL:** physical_world
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** cash_only · local_presence

Something findable but not pushed. The act of finding it is the payload. Digital classifieds get indexed; print creates a permanent physical artifact. The fact that anyone could theoretically see it makes it feel more real.

Personals section works differently than general classifieds — an ad written to qualities that describe the participant, with a phone number or instruction, implies something aimed at them specifically while being technically public.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant doesn't know to look and never finds it — only works as directed breadcrumb or ambient detail
- Paper edits or rejects unusual copy — keep language within normal classified conventions

---

### Physical Dead Drop
`id:physical_dead_drop` · `COST:varies` · `AUTONOMY:confederate` · `LEAD_TIME:2–3 days` · `INTENSITY:high`

**MECHANISMS:** hypervigilance · curiosity_exploration · you_ve_been_found
**ARC_FIT:** escalate · threshold · climax
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** High impact. Confirm participant's physical patterns with sponsor before deploying.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** qr_code · certified_mail · you_ve_been_found_inversion · briefed_confederate
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: elevated alertness, ritual satisfaction of finding — operative-level engagement · requires: participant in active investigation or game mode; must be motivated to look
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** operative retrieving something left specifically for them by someone who knew they'd come
**REINFORCE:** what was in the drop persists and can be referenced; subsequent communication that acknowledges what was found
**PERMISSION:** grants (physical-world play permission) · sequenced after participant has been primed to look for it
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** wonder · urgency
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** medium
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** constructed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** spy_fiction:high · arg:high
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** short
**REVERSIBILITY:** easy
**CHANNEL:** physical_world
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** local_presence

An object at a location they actually go implies prior surveillance of their movements. The experience has been in their physical world before they knew about it.

**Best placements:** object left with named person at their regular coffee shop, gym desk, or building concierge — "someone left this for you." The hospitality industry handles this constantly (proposals, surprises) and is discreet. Libraries and gyms are less cooperative; restaurants and hotels are more so.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Object moved or discarded before participant finds it — confirm timing; instruct the holder to keep it
- Someone else finds it — personally address the object clearly
- Participant doesn't frequent the location as reliably as expected — confirm patterns with sponsor

---

### QR Code on Physical Artifact
`id:qr_code` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:same day (agent); 2–3 days (physical placement)` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** curiosity_exploration · commitment_consistency (scanning is a choice)
**ARC_FIT:** any
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Increasing wariness about QR codes post-phishing awareness campaigns — include typed URL as fallback on any artifact.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** physical_dead_drop · certified_mail · notarized_doc · redacted_document
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** ephemeral
**SOMATIC:** induces: mild curiosity + low-stakes investigation arousal · requires: baseline; participant simply needs to notice and scan
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person who follows threads on their own initiative — an active participant rather than a passive recipient
**REINFORCE:** QR destination can update over the arc; participant may scan again; push notification on content update is possible if domain supports it
**PERMISSION:** grants · standalone
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** wonder
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** low
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** constructed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** escape_room:high · arg:high
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** immediate
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** physical_world
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill

URL behind the code can serve time-gated content: before the arc beat, a single cryptic line or holding image; after, the full revelation. Same physical code, living digital layer.

Use a domain you own in the URL rather than a QR redirect service — redirect services show the intermediary URL on some phones.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant doesn't scan (phishing concern) — always include typed fallback URL
- URL destination underwhelms — the physical artifact sets an expectation the digital layer must meet

---

### Annotation Layer
`id:annotation_layer` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** curiosity_exploration · social_proof · significance_quest
**ARC_FIT:** build · escalate
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** High `need_for_cognition` participants will engage deeply. May not be noticed by participants who don't examine pages carefully.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** real_domain · accidental_transmission · parallel_threads
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: investigative absorption + the sensation of finding a hidden conversation · requires: participant in investigation mode; must know what document to examine
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person who found what others reading the same document missed
**REINFORCE:** annotations can be updated with new layers; invisible ink version adds physical ritual; subsequent communication that references what was written
**PERMISSION:** grants · sequenced; participant must be directed toward the specific document first
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** wonder · disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** medium
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** constructed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:medium · escape_room:low
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** short
**REVERSIBILITY:** easy
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** multi-day
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill

Hypothesis (hypothes.is) is a web annotation platform that allows public annotations on any webpage. Annotations appear as highlighted text with comments visible to anyone using the Hypothesis extension or viewing via the Hypothesis reader.

Create an account as the fictional character. Annotate specific passages on the experience's own domain or on pages the participant will be directed to. The character has already been here — reading, highlighting, leaving notes — before the participant arrived.

The annotation layer creates a trail: evidence of a prior reader who cared about specific passages, disagreed with specific claims, connected specific ideas. This is distinct from the page's authored content — it's an additional layer that rewards close attention.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant doesn't have Hypothesis extension or notice annotations — direct them via a message: "read carefully, someone has been here before you"
- Annotations visible to general public — Hypothesis public annotations are searchable; use a private annotation group with the participant added, or accept the public nature as part of the design

---

## LEGITIMACY & SOCIAL PROOF

Plays that make the fictional world feel established — like it existed before this experience.

---

### LinkedIn Profile for Fictional Character
`id:linkedin_character` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:human_managed` · `LEAD_TIME:4 weeks minimum; 3+ months ideal` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** social_proof · trust_formation · parasocial_attachment
**ARC_FIT:** pre (build); open (referenced at first contact)
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Universal effect. The first thing most people check when they receive professional contact from an unknown person.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** ai_generated_face · real_domain · aged_social_media · press_release
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark
**SOMATIC:** induces: quiet background legitimacy (passive) · requires: participant to verify the character independently or be directed there
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person interacting with someone who has verifiable professional standing in the real world
**REINFORCE:** profile can be updated with new "activity" during arc; connections are static proof that persist indefinitely
**PERMISSION:** grants (character legitimacy) · pre-deploys; must be established before character makes first contact
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** warmth
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** none
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** never_solo
**REVERSIBILITY:** difficult
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill · ongoing_maintenance

Build at least 4 weeks before the experience. Key elements: posting history (irregular timing, mix of professional and personal), 50+ connections including mutual connections with the participant (requires advance work — send requests to second-degree connections), endorsements, consistent AI-generated profile photo. Human must manage connection requests; LinkedIn bot detection is sophisticated.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Account created too recently — visible in profile; no workaround except time
- Zero mutual connections — the first check most people do; invest in this
- AI face recognizable — use photorealistic style; test with colleagues before committing

---

### Google Maps Business Listing
`id:google_maps` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:2–4 weeks (verification)` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** social_proof · trust_formation
**ARC_FIT:** pre
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Background infrastructure.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** llc_registration · mailbox_address · real_domain
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark
**SOMATIC:** induces: background institutional legitimacy (passive) · requires: participant to look up the org address
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** investigator confirming a real physical location for the organization
**REINFORCE:** hours and photos can be adjusted; the listing persists; pairs with mailbox_address as a corroborating record
**PERMISSION:** grants (org physical-reality anchor) · pre-deploys alongside mailbox_address
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** none
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** never_solo
**REVERSIBILITY:** difficult
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill · ongoing_maintenance

A pin on the map. Participant who looks up the address finds a real business listing with photos, hours, description, and potentially reviews. Map-level reality is extremely convincing.

Google sends a postcard to the address for verification — confirm mailbox service forwards postcards. Add photos of the building exterior (Google Street View the Regus building), hours, a description in the organization's voice. Request reviews from secondary accounts.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Verification postcard not forwarded — confirm with mailbox service
- Google suspends listing for policy violations — keep category and description straightforward
- Street View shows a mailbox service storefront — acceptable; the existence of the listing is the main claim

---

### Press Release Distribution
`id:press_release` · `COST:mid` (~$75) · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:1–2 days` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** social_proof · illusory_truth_effect
**ARC_FIT:** pre · build
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Background infrastructure. Deploys when participant Googles the organization.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** real_domain · llc_registration · linkedin_character
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** document
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark · social
**SOMATIC:** induces: institutional recognition — the org has been noted by the real world · requires: participant directed to search or finding it naturally via Google
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person dealing with something that has had real media coverage
**REINFORCE:** a second press release at a later arc moment creates a timeline; the initial release is archived and findable by anyone indefinitely
**PERMISSION:** grants (media-reality validation) · pre-deploys; enables all org-contact plays to inherit news credibility
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** awe
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:low
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** long
**REVERSIBILITY:** difficult
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** cash_only · technical_skill

EIN Presswire ($50–100) indexes in Google News within 24–48 hours. The fictional organization's name now returns real news results. "As reported in industry news..." becomes technically accurate.

Write in standard wire format: dateline, FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE header, quote from fictional officer, boilerplate "About [Organization]", contact info (email on real domain, real phone number). Boringly credible content, not exciting content.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Content violates EIN Presswire guidelines — review before submitting; avoid certain claim types
- Participant knows how press release distribution works — acceptable for most

---

### Aged Social Media Account
`id:aged_social_media` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:human_managed` · `LEAD_TIME:3–6 months ideal` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** social_proof · parasocial_attachment · trust_formation
**ARC_FIT:** pre (build); build (participant discovers)
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Character voice must be established in advance and maintained consistently. Most effective for high `big_five_O` and high `need_for_cognition` participants who investigate characters they encounter.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** linkedin_character · ai_generated_face · real_domain
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark
**SOMATIC:** induces: mild credibility + investigative satisfaction at finding a real social trail · requires: participant looking into the character
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** investigator finding the character's social history — a public record of a person who has existed
**REINFORCE:** "recent" posts during the arc sustain character presence; participant may follow or bookmark the account
**PERMISSION:** grants (character social depth) · pre-deploys; most valuable when character is actively used in the arc
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** warmth · longing
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:medium
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** never_solo
**REVERSIBILITY:** difficult
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill · ongoing_maintenance

Twitter/X or Instagram. Post at irregular, human-seeming intervals. Content ratio: ~80% mundane professional/personal, ~20% narrative-adjacent. Follow and engage with real accounts. Have opinions. Characters without opinions don't read as real.

Buy 200–500 followers from a reputable service to clear the zero-credibility floor — this is standard practice and effective.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Platform suspension for bot-like patterns — vary timing; avoid automated tools that post at exact intervals
- Character voice inconsistency — establish a voice document before first post

---

### Consistent AI-Generated Character Face
`id:ai_generated_face` · `COST:free` (SD) or `low` (Midjourney) · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:1–2 hours` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** mere_exposure_effect · trust_formation (familiarity)
**ARC_FIT:** pre
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Universal. Consistency across platforms is the whole value.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** linkedin_character · aged_social_media · video_message · real_domain (team page)
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** personal
**SOMATIC:** induces: mild uncanny dissonance if examined closely — can't place why it feels slightly off · requires: participant examining character profile photos
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** investigator trying to place a face that doesn't quite connect to anyone they know
**REINFORCE:** same face used consistently across all platforms creates reliable recognition; may appear in other artifacts
**PERMISSION:** grants · pre-deploys with linkedin_character and aged_social_media
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** none
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** never_solo
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill

Generate with fixed seed parameter. Save the seed + full prompt — consistency requires identical parameters. Generate 20–30 variations: neutral, smiling, different angles, indoor/outdoor. Use photorealistic style; avoid oversaturated AI aesthetics.

Test across platforms — some compress images in ways that reveal AI generation artifacts.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Generation settings not saved — use different settings later, character face inconsistency
- AI face recognizable — use Midjourney --style raw or a photorealistic SD model; test with colleagues

---

### Reddit Account History
`id:reddit_history` · `COST:mid` (~$30 time investment, optional karma boosting) · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:1–4 weeks` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** social_proof · curiosity_exploration · parasocial_attachment
**ARC_FIT:** pre · build
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Primarily effective for participants who use Reddit or who would follow an investigation trail to Reddit. High `need_for_cognition` participants will read comment history.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** real_domain · aged_social_media · linkedin_character · annotation_layer
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark
**SOMATIC:** induces: investigative excitement — found the character's semi-private voice in an informal channel · requires: participant to search the character's name or org on Reddit
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** researcher who found the character's candid voice — not the official one
**REINFORCE:** new comments can be added during arc; replies from the account sustain the presence in that community
**PERMISSION:** grants (character informal depth) · pre-deploys or early arc
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** warmth · disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** medium
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** revealed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:medium
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** long
**REVERSIBILITY:** difficult
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill · ongoing_maintenance

A fictional character's Reddit comment history is a window into their inner life that LinkedIn and Instagram don't provide. Post in subreddits relevant to the character's background and the narrative theme. Include occasional narrative-relevant comments mixed with mundane participation.

Comment history is publicly visible and sorted by date — makes the character's "presence" over time concrete. Participants who investigate deeply will read it.

**Specific play:** the character comments on a post in a subreddit the participant frequents, before the experience begins. When the participant eventually looks them up, they find the character was in their community.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Reddit community detects non-genuine account — write in community-appropriate voice; contribute genuinely to threads before narrative content
- Account age too low — Reddit shows account age and low karma raises suspicion; start early

---

### GitHub Repository
`id:github_repo` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:2+ weeks for meaningful history` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** curiosity_exploration · social_proof · significance_quest (hidden messages)
**ARC_FIT:** pre · build · escalate
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Only effective with technically literate participants who would investigate a GitHub profile.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** real_domain · linkedin_character · annotation_layer
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark
**SOMATIC:** induces: investigative satisfaction + slight technical authority · requires: participant who uses GitHub or is directed there
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** investigator finding the technical footprint of something real and active
**REINFORCE:** commits can be added during the arc with narrative-relevant content; README can update
**PERMISSION:** grants (technical legitimacy) · pre-deploys; most effective for technical participants
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** wonder · disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** medium
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** revealed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:medium
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** long
**REVERSIBILITY:** difficult
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill · ongoing_maintenance

A GitHub account with a real commit history creates a character who works in code. The repository itself can contain:

- **Commit messages as narrative trail:** commit history is visible chronologically; messages can tell a story
- **Hidden messages in code comments:** content invisible at first glance that rewards reading
- **Variable and function names:** meaningful within the narrative
- **README files:** direct communication in the character's voice
- **Issues and PR comments:** evidence of collaboration or conflict with other (fictional) accounts
- **Code that actually does something:** even a simple working script establishes the character as real

The repository is findable via the character's name + GitHub, or via links in other profiles.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Commit timestamps don't match claimed timeline — set git commit timestamps explicitly when building history
- Code is clearly non-functional placeholder — write actual working code, even if simple

---

### Goodreads / Letterboxd Profile
`id:goodreads_profile` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:human_assist` · `LEAD_TIME:1 week` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** parasocial_attachment · curiosity_exploration
**ARC_FIT:** build · escalate
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** ramp
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Most effective when participant shares the platform. Reading/watching history provides intimate character knowledge.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** linkedin_character · aged_social_media · altered_book
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** world_mark
**SOMATIC:** induces: intimate familiarity — books reveal inner life in a way public platforms don't · requires: participant who uses Goodreads or is directed there
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person catching a glimpse of a character's intellectual and emotional interior
**REINFORCE:** new books can be "read" and reviewed during arc; the profile ages with activity
**PERMISSION:** grants (character intimacy) · pre-deploys; most effective when character is a sustained presence
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** warmth · longing
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** medium
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** borrowed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** arg:low
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** long
**REVERSIBILITY:** difficult
**CHANNEL:** digital_ambient
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill · ongoing_maintenance

A character's Goodreads or Letterboxd profile is among the most intimate self-portrait available online — what someone reads or watches reveals how they think and what they care about.

Build a reading/viewing history that's consistent with the character's background and the narrative themes. Reviews in the character's voice. A "currently reading" shelf with something meaningful. The participant who finds this knows the character better than anything the character has directly said.

**Specific play:** the character has reviewed a book that becomes important to the narrative. The review contains a clue, a framing, or information that predates the participant's involvement.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Platform not used by participant — less effective but still findable via investigation
- Review voice inconsistent with character's voice elsewhere

---

## TIMING & COINCIDENCE

Plays that create the sensation the experience knows where the participant is in time and space.

---

### Weather-Referenced Message
`id:weather_message` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:low`

**MECHANISMS:** hypervigilance · significance_quest (surveillance sensation)
**ARC_FIT:** any
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** spike
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Universal. Confirm participant's current city at intake — traveling participant breaks the effect.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** past_tense_timestamp · osint_personalization
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** ephemeral
**SOMATIC:** induces: slight disorientation — the message references their physical sensory reality at the exact moment of reading · requires: baseline; most effective in weather the participant actually notices
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person whose immediate physical environment is being watched — not just their digital behavior
**REINFORCE:** subsequent weather-referenced messages deepen ambient monitoring; forecast references ("you'll need an umbrella tomorrow") escalate predictive surveillance
**PERMISSION:** grants (ambient surveillance permission) · standalone
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** paranoia · disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** passive
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** revealed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** none
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** immediate
**REVERSIBILITY:** trivial
**CHANNEL:** email
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill

OpenWeatherMap free tier. Query at send time and insert one accurate real-time detail. One specific detail does disproportionate work. "We know the fog has been heavy this week" — true, specific, implies ongoing attention. Don't overexplain or call attention to it.

**FAILURE MODES:**
- Participant traveling — wrong weather completely breaks the effect; check with sponsor before deploying
- Effect reads as obvious API call to technical participants — acceptable for most

---

### Google Calendar Invite from Fictional Character
`id:calendar_invite` · `COST:free` · `AUTONOMY:agent` · `LEAD_TIME:same day` · `INTENSITY:medium`

**MECHANISMS:** commitment_consistency · significance_quest
**ARC_FIT:** threshold · escalate · climax scheduling
**BEAT_FUNCTION:** spike
**PARTICIPANT_NOTES:** Verify calendar platform at intake — check "scheduling preferences." High `need_for_closure` participants will feel strong pull to accept or decline.
**SYNERGIZES_WITH:** restaurant_reservation · fedex_tracking · real_domain (invite from org email)
**CONTRAINDICATED_AFTER:** none
**WITNESS_STRUCTURE:** none
**LANDSCAPE:** action
**LEGACY_SCOPE:** ephemeral
**SOMATIC:** induces: mild obligation + hypervigilance — their scheduling infrastructure has been accessed · requires: baseline
**IDENTITY_INVITE:** person whose schedule is known and is being scheduled against by something
**REINFORCE:** declining generates a follow-up; accepting creates the next beat; the event sits on their calendar for days
**PERMISSION:** grants · standalone
**EMOTIONAL_REGISTER:** urgency · disorientation
**AGENCY_DEMAND:** low
**FEEDBACK_TYPE:** none
**AGENCY_TYPE:** constructed
**FRAME_REQUIREMENT:** naive
**TROPE_RISK:** none
**DETECTION_WINDOW:** immediate
**REVERSIBILITY:** easy
**CHANNEL:** email
**DWELL_TIME:** brief
**REQUIRES:** technical_skill
